title: Indian Community Organizations
description: Associations, places of worship and cultural organizations for Indians in Belgium.

items.name: Brussels Mandir (Indian Cultural Centre asbl)
items.description: A Hindu temple established by the Indian community in Brussels, soft-opened in February 2012. Run by the Indian Cultural Centre asbl.

items.name: Ram Mandir Brussels
items.description: The oldest Hindu temple in Brussels, established over 30 years ago in Etterbeek.

items.name: Shankheshwar Parshvanath Jain Temple
items.description: A Jain temple serving Antwerp's Gujarati Jain community, many of whom trace their roots to Palanpur, Gujarat, and work in the diamond trade.

items.name: BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir Antwerp
items.description: Part of the international BAPS Swaminarayan network, serving the Hindu community in Antwerp.

items.name: Gurdwara Sangat Sahib
items.description: The largest gurudwara in Belgium, built in 2021 in traditional Indian architectural style with capacity for around 1,000 worshippers. Sint-Truiden and the surrounding Haspengouw region are home to Belgium's largest Sikh community, historically drawn by seasonal fruit-picking work.

items.name: Gurdwara Guru Nanak Sahib Cultureel Centrum
items.description: Established in 1999 in a converted cinema, serving the Sikh community in and around Brussels.

items.name: Indian Association of Belgium
items.description: A community association aiming to celebrate Indian cultural heritage and foster connections between Indians and Belgians.

items.name: Antwerp Indian Association
items.description: Founded in 1979, representing the Indian community in Antwerp, historically closely tied to the diamond trade.

items.name: ABC – A Bollywood Company
items.description: Belgium's only Indian-led Indian dance company, led by artistic director Swapnil Dagliya, offering Bollywood, Bhangra, Garba and Indian folk dance performances and classes. Organises the annual Gent India Dans Festival (GIDF).

items.name: Bollylicious
items.description: An Indian dance collective founded by four Belgian dancers, presenting Bollywood and fusion Indian dance across Belgium.

items.name: International Protestant Church of Brussels (IPC)
items.description: An international, interdenominational congregation attended by many Indian Christian families in Brussels; no dedicated Indian-specific congregation was identified, so this and other international churches are the most common option for Indian Christians in the city.
